journey.title: From Fullstack Systems to AI Agent Architecture
journey.intro: I bring production experience delivering React, Next.js, TypeScript, GraphQL, fintech, insurance, payments, and banking systems. I am now transitioning toward AI Systems Engineering and Agent Architecture through hands-on work in RAG, MCP tooling, agent orchestration, and evaluation.

positioning.transition: My AI transition is active and real, but still in progress.
positioning.scope: I avoid exaggerating AI seniority and focus on measurable execution and learning velocity.

hero.description: React / Next.js / TypeScript engineer with production experience across fintech, banking, and payment platforms. Currently focused on AI agents, RAG pipelines, evaluation frameworks, and production reliability.

projects.calloutDescription: The same production engineering principles behind these projects — clear architecture, rigorous testing, and observability — are now being applied to AI agents, RAG pipelines, and evaluation frameworks.

principles.title: How I build systems
principles.subtitle: Principles shaped by years of shipping production software.

about.title: About
about.bio1: I'm Moisés Escudero, a Software Engineer from Caracas, Venezuela, with a degree from Universidad Católica Andrés Bello. Over the past years I've built production systems across fintech, banking, insurance, crowdfunding, parking automation, and payment platforms — primarily with React, Next.js, TypeScript, GraphQL, MongoDB, Java, and Firebase.
about.bio2: I'm now channeling that production engineering background into AI systems. The same principles that keep financial platforms reliable — clear architecture, rigorous testing, observability, and explicit failure handling — apply directly to building trustworthy AI agents, RAG pipelines, and evaluation frameworks. This is not a pivot away from engineering; it's the next layer of it.

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.subtitle: Based in Caracas, Venezuela. Open to remote opportunities across LATAM, the US, and Europe. Interested in working together or have a question? Drop me a message.

lines.ai: Evolving into AI agents, RAG & MCP

strengths.leadership: Leadership & teamwork  coordinating cross-functional delivery in distributed teams.

react.label: React + TypeScript
react.description: Years of production experience in type-safe frontend systems.

fintech.label: Fintech & Payments
fintech.description: Banking, crowdfunding, insurance, and payment platforms.

fullstack.label: Fullstack Delivery
fullstack.description: End-to-end systems with GraphQL, MongoDB, Java, and Firebase.

ai.label: AI Systems (Next)
ai.description: Building toward RAG, agents, MCP, and evaluation frameworks.

crowdfunding.name: Crowdfunding Platform
crowdfunding.description: Full-featured investment crowdfunding platform with campaign management, investor dashboards, KYC flows, and real-time funding progress. Served thousands of active users in production.

parking.name: Parking Automation System
parking.description: IoT-integrated parking management system handling gate control, license plate recognition, payment processing, and occupancy tracking across multiple locations.

insurance.name: Insurance Quoting Engine
insurance.description: Remote engagement with a US-based insurance company. Built a dynamic quoting system with multi-step forms, risk calculations, and real-time premium estimation for end consumers.

banking.name: Banking & Payroll Systems
banking.description: Core banking modules for payroll disbursement, transaction reconciliation, and reporting. Built with Java and integrated with legacy banking infrastructure.

gateway.name: Payment Gateway & Crypto E-commerce
gateway.description: Custom payment gateway supporting fiat and cryptocurrency transactions. Integrated checkout flows, webhook-based settlement, and merchant dashboards.

rag.name: RAG Document Search
rag.description: A retrieval-augmented generation pipeline with citation extraction. Query a knowledge base and inspect how chunks are ranked and referenced.

tools.name: Tool Calling Sandbox
tools.description: An agent that picks and executes tools through the Model Context Protocol. Inspect payloads, permissions, and return values in real time.

evals.name: Agent Evaluation Harness
evals.description: Run property-based tests and regression suites against agent outputs. Compare prompt versions and track quality metrics over time.

productionFirst.title: Production-first mindset
productionFirst.description: Every feature is built for real users from day one. Performance, error handling, and edge cases are part of the first commit, not an afterthought.

architecture.title: Clear architecture over shortcuts
architecture.description: Well-defined boundaries between layers, explicit data flows, and readable code matter more than clever abstractions. Systems should be easy to reason about.

communication.title: Communication as a technical skill
communication.description: Documenting decisions, writing clear PRs, and explaining trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders is as important as writing the code itself.

testing.title: Testing & maintainability
testing.description: Automated tests, type safety, and CI pipelines catch regressions before they reach users. Code that cannot be tested confidently cannot be shipped confidently.

aiEvolution.title: Evolving toward AI-driven systems
aiEvolution.description: Applying the same rigor from production engineering to AI: grounded outputs, tool safety, evaluation suites, and cost-aware inference.

transition.title: From fintech frontends to AI agents: why the transition makes sense
transition.excerpt: Production systems taught me reliability, testing, and architecture. Those same principles apply directly to building trustworthy AI agents.

evalFirst.title: Evaluation-first development for LLM applications
evalFirst.excerpt: Shipping a prompt without an eval suite is like deploying without tests. A practical framework for measuring agent quality.

mcp.title: What the Model Context Protocol means for tool-based agents
mcp.excerpt: MCP standardizes how agents discover and call tools. Notes on schema validation, auth scoping, and composability.
